filename: # Fill one row per photo you drop into pilot/images/.
# Columns:
#   filename         — basename of the image file (e.g. "onesie_01.jpg"). Must match exactly.
#   tag_visibility   — how much of the tag is in the frame:
#                        visible — tag fills a good chunk of frame
#                        partial — tag is in frame but small
#                        none    — no tag in frame at all (pure garment photo).
#                      For `none` rows
tag_visibility: the harness grades a null brand/size prediction as

filename: #                      CORRECT — the model can't read a tag that isn't in the shot
#                      user fills those fields in manually. Non-null wrong predictions on a
#                      `none` row count as hallucinations (the thing we most want to avoid).
#                      Category and item_type are graded normally in all visibility buckets —
#                      the model should always be able to tell a onesie from a pair of pants.
#   brand            — brand name as printed on the tag (or as you know it — even if the
#                      tag isn't in frame
tag_visibility: fill this in for your own records). Leave blank

filename: #                      if unbranded. Only graded strictly when tag_visibility is visible/partial.
#   size             — canonical enum: 0-3M | 3-6M | 6-9M | 9-12M | 12-18M | 18-24M
#   category         — canonical enum: tops_and_bodysuits | one_pieces | bottoms |
#                      dresses_and_skirts | outerwear | sleepwear | footwear |
#                      accessories | swimwear
#   item_type        — canonical slot: bodysuits | day_tops | one_pieces | shorts |
#                      pants_leggings | dresses | sleep_sacks | pajamas | rain_gear |
#                      jackets | socks | shoes | hats | mittens | bibs | burp_cloths |
#                      swimwear
#   notes            — free text for you (ignored by the harness). Useful for flagging
#                      tricky cases like "tag worn
#                      is a logo
# What a good pilot set looks like: ~5 tag-visible
# That's the distribution that most resembles what parents will actually photograph.
# Delete these comment lines before running the eval — the CSV reader treats them as data.
# Examples:
# onesie_01.jpg
# sleeper_02.jpg
# dress_03.jpg
